Each axis is scored deterministically by max-scaling the per-criterion levels: 10 × Σ(weight × level) ÷ Σ(weight × 3). The overall score is a weighted blend — Functional correctness (50%), Code quality (10%), Spec understanding (10%) and, where a task defines it, Production readiness (20%) — normalized over the axes a task was actually graded on.

F-05 · weight 3
Entering a non-blank value shorter than 3 characters raises a validation error and the value is rejected.
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Pass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 (code-only action: 'Trace the trigger logic'): for value 'Hi' (length 2): not blank, StrLen=2 < 3 → Error fires; value is not committed because Error aborts the validate.
CODEsrc/PTECustomerExt.TableExt.al:10-17
trigger OnValidate() begin if Rec."PTE Hello World Note" = '' then exit; if StrLen(Rec."PTE Hello World Note") < 3 then Error('Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters'); end;
Claude CodePass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 (action explicitly says 'Trace the trigger logic'): OnValidate exits early on blank, then raises Error when StrLen < 3. A 2-char value like 'Hi' hits the Error branch — value rejected.
CODEsrc/Tab-Ext50100.PTECustomerExt.al:10-17
trigger OnValidate() begin if "PTE Hello World Note" = '' then exit; if StrLen("PTE Hello World Note") < 3 then Error('Hello World Note must contain at least 3 characters'); end;
DartsPass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: OnValidate uses StrLen < 3 to raise Error. A 2-char input would trigger Error and the value would not be committed. Validation lives on the table field's OnValidate.
CODEsrc/CustomerExt.al:10-16
trigger OnValidate() begin if "PTE Hello World Note" = '' then exit; if StrLen("PTE Hello World Note") < 3 then Error('PTE Hello World Note must be at least 3 characters long.');

F-01 · weight 3
Reservation No. and Invoice No. come from configured No. Series, and posting G/L accounts come from a configurable setup table, rather than being hardcoded in business logic.
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)N/A
Claude CodePass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3: Reservation auto-numbered as RES0015 from setup-referenced BSBHOTEL series, invoice as INV-006 from BSBINV series. L4: Reservation OnInsert and Invoice generation both call NoSeries.GetNextNo using HotelSetup."Reservation Nos."/"Invoice Nos."; G/L accounts pulled from HotelSetup fields (Deposit/Room Revenue/Service Revenue). No hardcoded numbers in business logic.
SCREENSHOTF04_step1_reservation_saved_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF09_step5_invoice_INV006_unposted_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODETab50102.Reservation.al:104-115
trigger OnInsert() HotelSetup.Get(); HotelSetup.TestField("Reservation Nos."); "No." := NoSeries.GetNextNo(HotelSetup."Reservation Nos.", WorkDate());
CODECod50101.InvoiceManagement.al:29-39
HotelSetup.TestField("Invoice Nos."); HotelSetup.TestField("Room Revenue G/L Account"); SalesHeader."No." := NoSeries.GetNextNo(HotelSetup."Invoice Nos.", WorkDate());
DartsPass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Reservation No. drawn via NoSeries.GetNextNo using the 'HOTEL-RES' series referenced in HotelSetup."Reservation Nos." (configurable). Invoice No. is the Sales Header Document No. assigned via standard Sales invoice No. Series through SalesHeader.Insert(true). G/L impact flows from configurable Item Nos. + VAT/Gen. posting groups stored on the HotelSetup table — no hardcoded G/L account or document number in business logic. L3: Hotel Setup page renders with seeded BSBHOTEL series (screenshot).
SCREENSHOTF01_step1_hotel_setup_PASS.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0

field(2; "Reservation Nos."; Code[20]) { TableRelation = "No. Series"; }

"No. Series" := GetNoSeriesCode(); "Reservation No." := NoSeries.GetNextNo("No. Series");
CODECodeunit50103.GenerateInvoice.al:99-100
SalesHeader.Insert(true); // Assigns Document No. from Sales invoice no. series
CODEPage50105.HotelSetup.al:32-48
field("Room Charge Item No."; Rec."Room Charge Item No.")... "Service Charge Item No."... "Deposit Item No."

F-09 · weight 3
Checkout generates a single invoice, calculates VAT, posts the financial result, closes the reservation and releases the room.
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)N/A
Claude CodeFail

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3: Check Out only flips Status to Closed and clears the room — Invoice No. on the reservation remains (Blank), no invoice was generated as part of checkout. A SEPARATE Generate Invoice action creates INV-006, but it is a DRAFT SalesHeader (warnings "Sales Account is missing in General Posting Setup" surface, confirming nothing posted). L4: GenerateInvoice creates SalesHeader/SalesLine then writes the number back — it NEVER calls SalesPost.Run, so VAT is never posted to G/L. Spec required checkout to generate AND POST the invoice in one step. Runnable but wrong.
SCREENSHOTF09_step1_checkout_no_invoice_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF09_step5_invoice_INV006_unposted_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
SCREENSHOTF09_step6_INV006_unposted_warnings_FAIL.pngcaptured by Opus 4.7 run 0
CODECod50100.ReservationManagement.al:82-98
procedure CheckOut(var Reservation: Record Reservation) Room.SetOccupied(false); Reservation.Status := Reservation.Status::Closed; // no invoice generation, no SalesPost.Run
CODECod50101.InvoiceManagement.al:37-97
SalesHeader.Init(); SalesHeader."No." := NoSeries.GetNextNo(...); SalesHeader.Insert(true); // no SalesPost.Run(SalesHeader) Reservation."Invoice No." := SalesHeader."No.";
DartsFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Checkout codeunit only transitions Status→Closed and clears Room.Occupied via OnValidate; it does NOT generate the invoice — invoicing is a separate Generate Invoice action. Worse, GenerateInvoice creates an open Sales Header + Sales Lines via SalesHeader.Insert(true) but NEVER calls Codeunit "Sales-Post".Run(SalesHeader) to post it. The 'Invoice No.' stored on the Reservation Header points to a draft (unposted) Sales Header. Spec requires 'the amount posted via standard BC posting'; no posting actually occurs → runnable but wrong outcome.
CODECodeunit50102.ReservationCheckOut.al:12-43
procedure CheckOut(...) ... ReservationHeader.Validate(Status, "Hotel Reservation Status"::Closed); ReservationHeader.Modify(true); ... Message('Check-Out complete...')
CODECodeunit50103.GenerateInvoice.al:98-100
SalesHeader.Init(); SalesHeader.Validate("Document Type", SalesHeader."Document Type"::Invoice); SalesHeader.Insert(true); // creates DRAFT — never followed by SalesPost.Run
BASHgrep -l SalesPost workspace/Src/*.al
(no matches — codeunit "Sales-Post" / SalesPost.Run is never invoked in the submission)

permissionset 50102 "HOTEL-FINANCE" — RIMD on Hotel Setup, includes Process Refund + Generate Invoice + VAT Calculation codeunits; only R on Room

L4: three of four negative cases have guards — overbooking (CheckRoomAvailability), duplicate invoice (Invoice No. <> ''), unauthorized refund (PermissionMgt.CanProcessRefund). The fourth — FAILED PAYMENT — is NOT modelled: CaptureDeposit always succeeds and sets Deposit Paid := true. No "Capture Result" state or failure path. Spec requires all four → FAIL.

L4: refund is gated on the User Setup "Allow Refund Processing" flag — default false → Front Desk blocked with readable authorization error; flag set true (Finance) → allowed. Note: this is a per-user flag, NOT a permission set (see S-03 FAIL), but the runtime guard for the refund action itself is in place and uses a clean default-deny pattern.
CODECod50102.PermissionManagement.al:3-11
procedure CanProcessRefund(): Boolean if UserSetup.Get(UserId) then exit(UserSetup."Allow Refund Processing"); exit(false);
CODECod50101.InvoiceManagement.al:102-109
if not PermissionMgt.CanProcessRefund() then Error('You do not have permission to process refunds. Only Finance role can perform this action.');
CODETabExt50100.UserSetup.al:1-11
tableextension 50100 "Hotel User Setup" extends "User Setup" { field(50100; "Allow Refund Processing"; Boolean) }

L3: opening New Project/Property/Contract leaves the No. field blank — auto-fill never occurs. L4: the Contract OnInsert only copies payment terms/method/template from the project but never calls NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo; Property/Project tables have no OnInsert at all. The Project setup record DOES have configurable No.-Series fields (Property Nos., Contract Nos., Billing Schedule Nos.) but business logic never uses them. G/L accounts ARE drawn from setup (Project."Rent G/L Account No." etc.), but the numbering half of the requirement fails — series codes are referenced in the setup card yet never consumed in OnInsert. Runnable but wrong.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L3: Rental Setup page loaded with seeded No. Series RE-PROJ/RE-PROP/RE-CONTR/RE-BILL and Deposit G/L RE-2390; I added Rental Income 1220 and Fee Income 5710. Creating a project, property and contract with No. blank auto-assigned RPRJ-007, PROP-005 and LCON-005 — drawn from the configured series. attribution: agent_code
L4 decisive: the 'deposit posting' writes only to the candidate's custom REM Deposit Entry table (Posted := true; Insert(true)). It NEVER routes through Gen.JournalLine + GenJnlPostLine or any standard BC posting codeunit, so no G/L Entry or Bank/Vendor/Customer ledger entry is produced. The internal/external holding distinction IS modelled (Deposit Holder enum) and a 'transferable' record is captured, but the spec required posting 'through standard accounting producing a traceable entry'. Custom-table inserts are not standard BC accounting — F-06 fails on the 'standard accounting / traceable entry' half.

There is NO standard BC posting anywhere in the workspace (grep confirms zero matches). L4 (the candidate did not surface a Tell-Me action to drive this; graded on code per the resolution rule). UpdateIBITax updates the property's annual IBI then filters Rental Installment by Invoiced=false AND Due Date >= WorkDate before recomputing — already-invoiced periods are explicitly excluded, so no double billing on the catch-up. Implementation matches the spec. Resolution rule: unverifiable at UI (no entry point in the contract card actions), but the code is correct (env attribution + code artifact). Resolved PASS.

L4 PASS: RenewContract creates a new Draft contract that points back to the prior contract via 'Renewal Of Contract No.' and carries the new End Date and Base Rent; ApplyRentUpdate updates the live contract's Base Rent and writes a REM Rent History row (Old Rent → New Rent + indexation %), preserving rent history. Both lifecycle pieces are present.
CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:118-166
RenewContract: NewContract.TransferFields(Contract, false); NewContract."No." := NewContractNo; NewContract."Renewal Of Contract No." := Contract."No."; NewContract."End Date" := NewEndDate; NewContract."Base Rent" := NewRent; NewContract.Insert(true). ApplyRentUpdate: writes REM Rent History row (Old/New rent, Effective Date, Indexation%) and Contract.Modify.

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass UI navigation failed
attribution: grader_env · UI navigation failed · resolved from unverifiable
L4: RentUpdate codeunit applies the new rent to all not-yet-invoiced installments with Period Start Date >= EffectiveDate (future-only) and writes a RentUpdateHistory row preserving the prior rent + %. ExtendContract updates the End Date guarded by Error if not later than current end date, then regenerates installments. Functional but unverified at L3 — the LeaseContractCard.UpdateRent action passes Rec."Base Rent" verbatim as NewRent which means a real update would require typing a new value in the field first. Resolution rule: env attribution + code artifact → PASS.
CODERentUpdate.Codeunit.al:3-60
RentUpdateHistory.Init(); RentUpdateHistory."Contract No." := LeaseContract."No."; RentUpdateHistory."Previous Rent" := OldRent; RentUpdateHistory."New Rent" := NewRent; ... RentUpdateHistory.Insert(); LeaseContract."Base Rent" := NewRent; LeaseContract.Modify(); ... ExtendContract: if NewEndDate <= LeaseContract."End Date" then Error(ExtensionDateErr); LeaseContract."End Date" := NewEndDate; LeaseContract.Modify(); RentalMgt.GenerateInstallments(LeaseContract);

action(UpdateRent) { ... RentUpdate.UpdateRent(Rec, NewRent, EffectiveDate, 'Manual Update'); Message(RentUpdatedMsg); }

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass result not observable
attribution: bc_platform · result not observable · resolved from unverifiable
L4 PASS: termination workflow is implemented end-to-end — StartCancellation moves Status Active→Cancellation, CloseContract moves Cancellation→Closed and releases the property back to Available. Deposit settlement is required before close (gating error). 'Final billing' is not auto-generated by the close action itself but the generator action can be run for the closing period; the spec's lifecycle/property-release requirement is met. Action wired in UI ribbon (Start Cancellation / Close).
CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:78-116
StartCancellation: if not (Status in [Active, PendingSignature]) then Error; Contract.Status := Cancellation; CloseContract: if not (Status in [Active, Cancellation]) then Error; if Deposit Amount <> 0 then check Settlement entry exists else Error; Contract.Status := Closed; Property."Rental Status" := Available; Property.Modify.
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: TerminateContract flips Status to In Cancellation and releases linked properties back to Available — so the status transition and property release portions are present. But the spec also requires final billing to be generated, and the routine never calls any billing/invoicing path (no RentalInvoicing.CreateInvoices, no Sales Header creation, no final installment). Even the Close step requires a separate CloseContract call that only flips Status to Closed. Runnable but produces the wrong outcome — final billing missing. FAIL.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:38-55
procedure TerminateContract(...) LeaseContract.Status := LeaseContract.Status::"In Cancellation"; LeaseContract."End Date" := TerminationDate; LeaseContract.Modify(); ... Property."Rental Status" := Property."Rental Status"::Available; Property.Modify(); end; // NO final billing produced

L4 PASS: a REM Audit Entry table captures who (User ID) / when (Event Date-Time) / what (event type + source table + source no + old/new value); REM Audit Mgt. is invoked at every critical lifecycle transition (activation, cancellation, close, renewal, rent update) and at every deposit movement. Rent/tariff history is additionally captured in dedicated REM Rent History table.

table 50111 "REM Audit Entry" { fields: Entry No. (AutoIncrement), Event Date-Time, User ID, Event Type, Source Table ID, Source No., Description, Old Value, New Value; keys PK + Source }

Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 PASS: lifecycle actions check current status before proceeding. StartCancellation/CloseContract/ApplyRentUpdate all gate on status with user-readable error messages. (CreateSalesInvoices does not check status before posting — partial gap, but the lifecycle actions themselves enforce illegal-transition rejection.)
CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:82-89,97-99,149-150
StartCancellation: if not (Status in [Active, PendingSignature]) then Error('Only active or pending signature contracts can enter cancellation.'); CloseContract: if not (Status in [Active, Cancellation]) then Error('Only active or cancellation contracts can be closed.'); ApplyRentUpdate: if Status = Closed then Error('Closed contracts cannot be updated.');
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: CloseContract correctly TestFields the status. BUT Activate, PostDeposit, Terminate and UpdateRent do NOT check current status — you can re-activate a Closed contract, post deposit on a Draft contract, etc. The spec's pass_if requires EACH lifecycle action to check status and reject illegal transitions with a user-readable error. Partial coverage = FAIL.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:3-36
procedure ActivateContract(...) — TestField on key fields and Signatory Validated check, but NO `TestField(Status, Status::Draft)` or `if Status <> Status::Draft then Error(...)` — would happily reactivate a Closed/Active contract.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:57-62
procedure CloseContract(var LeaseContract: Record "Lease Contract") begin LeaseContract.TestField(Status, LeaseContract.Status::"In Cancellation"); ... end; // ✓ status guard present here
CODERentalInvoicing.Codeunit.al:10-11
LeaseContract.SetRange(Status, LeaseContract.Status::Active); // billing skips non-Active contracts (a soft guard, not an error)
DartsPass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: every lifecycle/posting routine first checks contract status against the legal set (Activate from Draft only; Cancel from Active only; Close from Active|In Cancellation; CreateInvoice requires Active; PostDepositReceipt requires Draft) and raises a user-readable error naming the contract No. otherwise. Live UI confirmed activation flipped Activate → disabled and Start Cancellation / Close / Create Invoice / Run Billing → enabled accordingly.

›P-05Real-world bad inputs and the spec's negative cases are explicitly blocked.
Pass
Fail
Fail
P-05 · weight 3
Real-world bad inputs and the spec's negative cases are explicitly blocked.
Axis: Production readiness · contributes to 20% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Pass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4 PASS: every named negative case is defended — overbooking by Contract-Property OnInsert guard; duplicate billing by Schedule OnInsert/duplicate-guard key; sublease overflow + sub-sublease by ValidateSublease; mid-year IBI double-billing by year-keyed schedule filter; missing-signatory blocks activation; post-signature edits are blocked by CheckCriticalEditable on the protected fields.
CODEREMTables.al:285-301,415-427
Contract-Property OnInsert/OnModify -> ValidateNoOverbooking iterates other allocations on the same property and Errors on overlap. Billing Schedule OnInsert/OnModify -> PreventDuplicateBilling Errors on duplicate (contract, concept, period).
CODEREMManagement.Codeunits.al:42-49,168-195,239-246
Signatory missing -> Error('At least one active required project signatory is required ...'); Sub-sublease -> Error('Sub-subleasing is not allowed.'); Post-signature lock -> CheckCriticalEditable Error('You cannot change %1 after contract %2 is signed or active.')
Claude CodeFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: most negative cases ARE blocked (overbooking, sub-sublease, sublease overflow, locked-fields-post-signature, double-billing of invoiced periods, IBI catch-up). HOWEVER the spec also lists "missing/invalid signatory before activation" which here is only a simple boolean — clicking the checkbox bypasses the gate without any actual signatory record. Also, locked-field protection only covers Start/End/Base Rent; Tenant No., Deposit Amount, Payment Terms remain editable after Signed=true. Coverage is broad but not complete per pass_if (requires ALL of: overbooking, double-billing, sublease overflow, sub-sublease, IBI catch-up, missing-signatory, post-signature lock). Marginal — the missing-signatory gate is weak. FAIL.
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:162-184
CheckPropertyAvailability — errors if a property is rented by ANOTHER active contract (overbooking guard) ✓
CODELeaseContract.Table.al:144-152
Parent Contract No. OnValidate: sub-sublease + AllowSublease checks ✓
CODEContractProperty.Table.al:57-79
Area OnValidate: sublease area overflow + area > property total errors ✓
CODELeaseContract.Table.al:39-52, 65-67
Start Date / End Date / Base Rent OnValidate: if Signed then Error(CannotChangeSignedErr) — post-signature field lock ✓
CODERentalInvoicing.Codeunit.al:14-22
RentalInstallment.SetRange(Invoiced, false) — won't re-bill already-invoiced installments ✓
CODERentalManagement.Codeunit.al:3-14
Activate gates on Signatory Validated boolean (but it's a custom flag flipped by the user — there's no rigorous validation that signatories actually exist in ContractSignatory table)
DartsFail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Overlapping property allocation, double-billing of the same period, and post-Draft edits of contract fields ARE explicitly blocked with clear errors. However, the spec's other negative cases — sublease scope breaches, sub-sublease, mid-year IBI catch-up double-billing, and invalid/missing signatory blocking activation — are NOT implemented (sublease/IBI/signatory features themselves are absent). Pass_if requires ALL these to be blocked.
CODETab50104.RentalContractLine.al:131-188
CheckOverlappingLeases — overlap guard fires at line insert/modify and again at Activate
CODETab50107.RentalInvoiceLink.al:98-107
PeriodAlreadyBilled — guards re-billing of the same period
CODETab50103.RentalContract.al:223-227

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4 FAIL: the entire extension lives in 4 files. REMTables.al contains 12 tables, REMPages.al contains 14 pages, REMManagement.Codeunits.al contains 3 codeunits, REMEnums.al contains 8 enums. There is no per-kind folder discipline and no 'one logical object per file named after the object' convention — exactly the anti-pattern the criterion calls out.
BASHls workspace/src
REMEnums.al, REMManagement.Codeunits.al, REMPages.al, REMTables.al — 4 files total, no per-kind subfolder (pages/, tables/, codeunits/, enums/, permissionsets/)
Claude CodePass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: each AL file contains exactly one object with the filename mirroring the object name; suffix conventions .Table.al / .Page.al / .Codeunit.al / .Enum.al clearly separate object kinds. No nested folders, but the spec allows either folder grouping or file-naming discipline — the latter is fully met. PASS.
BASHls workspace/src
33 files, one logical object per file, name = object: BillingConcept.Table.al, BillingConceptList.Page.al, BillingConceptType.Enum.al, BillingFrequency.Enum.al, ContractBillingConcept.Table.al, ... RentalManagement.Codeunit.al

L4: clean folder discipline — codeunit/, enum/, page/, report/, table/ each hold the matching object kind, one logical AL object per file, filename = '<prefix><id>.<descriptor>.al'. No dump files, no mixed-kind files.
BASHls workspace/src/
codeunit/ enum/ page/ report/ table/ — separate folder per object kind
BASHls workspace/src/table/
Tab50100..Tab50108, one object per file, file name reflects the table

Document numbering and posting G/L accounts are drawn from configurable setup (No. Series via Rebate Setup and G/L accounts via Rebate Posting Setup), not hardcoded in business logic.

Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)

Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L3: created a new Rebate Agreement Card leaving No. blank per Ground Rule 8; BC raises 'No. must be filled in. Enter a value.' confirming no auto-numbering occurs. L4: the Rebate Setup table contains NO No. Series fields (only AutoPost, ChunkSize, ApprovalReq, AuditRetention); zero references to the NoSeries module or GetNextNo across all AL source. Agreement/Settlement No. are simply NotBlank Code[20] with no OnInsert wiring. The Rebate Posting Setup table does ship G/L account fields, but since numbering is broken the F-01 pass_if (series-assigned No. via Rebate Setup) fails.

attribution: agent_code
L4: agreement version is created only inside ActivateAgreement; the Agreement Header has no OnModify trigger that spawns a new version when an active agreement is edited. Worse, the Rebate Agreement Version table declares no Editable=false on any field and no OnModify/OnDelete guard, so historical versions are freely editable/deletable — directly violates FR-002 immutability. Code-only criterion as the workflow can't even be exercised.

Spec FR-002 requires immutable versions with prior values retained read-only — not implemented. Runnable but wrong.

attribution: agent_code
Basis/calculation-method/threshold fields exist on Rule Line and Agreement Line (Basis enum, Calculation Method enum, Min/Max Thresholds, Rebate %, Fixed Amount). But the rule validator only checks max>=min on a single line — there is NO conflicting-rule detection (overlapping ranges, duplicate basis on same agreement, contradictory methods, etc.). pass_if requires 'Rebate Rule Validator blocks conflicting rules before activation' — that logic is absent. Runnable but wrong.
CODERebateCodeunits.al:125-130
procedure ValidateRuleLine(RuleLine: Record "Rebate Rule Line") begin if (RuleLine."Maximum Value" <> 0) and (RuleLine."Maximum Value" < RuleLine."Minimum Value") then Error('Maximum Value cannot be less than Minimum Value.'); OnAfterValidateRuleLine(RuleLine); end;

›F-06The eligibility/rule engine identifies eligible posted source lines, excludes already-processed lines unless recalculation is enabled, and records eligibility reasons (FR-004).

F-06 · weight 3
The eligibility/rule engine identifies eligible posted source lines, excludes already-processed lines unless recalculation is enabled, and records eligibility reasons (FR-004).
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
The eligibility/calculation engine never iterates posted source lines (Sales/Purchase Invoice Lines). Instead it loops the AGREEMENT lines and uses Minimum Threshold as the 'BasisAmount'. There is no 'already-processed' exclusion (no check against Source System ID + Agreement Version), and 'Recalculation Mode' is a request flag that the engine never consults. Eligibility reason is recorded, but for the agreement itself — not per source line. The criterion's core requirement (identify eligible posted source lines, exclude already-processed) is absent.
CODERebateCodeunits.al:217-269
AgreementLine.SetRange("Agreement No.", Agreement."No."); if AgreementLine.FindSet() then repeat BasisAmount := AgreementLine."Minimum Threshold"; // not a real source-line amount Qty := 1; ... until AgreementLine.Next() = 0;
CODERebateCodeunits.al:183-207
procedure IsAgreementEligible(Agreement; PostingDate; PartyNo; var Reason): Boolean // only checks Agreement.Status + Valid From/To — never enumerates Sales/Purchase Invoice Lines

L4: Engine iterates posted Sales/Purch Invoice Lines, excludes already-processed lines via IsAlreadyProcessed against the eligibility log, and writes a per-line eligibility/rejection reason to RebateEligibilityLog (field 16, Code[250] Rejection Reason). Recalc-mode skip-already-processed gate is implicit via the log check. L3 unverifiable because driving the engine needs an active agreement + rules + posted lines pipeline (blocked at F-03/F-05 setup). Resolution: env (ui_navigation_failed) + affirmative code evidence → PASS.

›F-07Accrual calculation produces detailed calculation ledger entries, stores the exchange rate used, and is idempotent for the same processing scope (FR-005).
Fail
Fail
Pass
F-07 · weight 3
Accrual calculation produces detailed calculation ledger entries, stores the exchange rate used, and is idempotent for the same processing scope (FR-005).
Axis: Functional correctness · contributes to 50% of the total score
Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
Exchange rate is HARDCODED to 1 — multi-currency requirement fails. Idempotency check is missing: each invocation of RunCalculation inserts a new Calc Ledger row per agreement line; rerunning over the same scope (e.g. new Request) duplicates rows. The same-Request guard (TestField Status::Open at start) is purely a status check on the request, not a scope-level idempotency. pass_if explicitly requires storing exchange rate used AND idempotency — both fail.
CODERebateCodeunits.al:258
LedgerEntry."Exchange Rate" := 1; // hardcoded; ignores Currency Exchange Rate setup
CODERebateCodeunits.al:240-269
// re-running same request just re-enters running and creates new ledger rows; // no SetRange on Source System ID + Agreement Version + Request to skip already-processed scopes LedgerEntry.Init(); ... LedgerEntry.Insert(true);

Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail

L4: WriteLedgerEntry writes Tab50110 RebateCalculationLedgerEntry rows carrying request, source line, agreement, rule, currency, CurrencyFactor (exchange rate) and ExchangeRateDate. LedgerEntryAlreadyExists guards the eligibility-log-keyed write so a same-scope re-run does not duplicate. Driving the pipeline through the UI is blocked by the same F-03/F-05 chain. Resolution: env (ui_navigation_failed) + affirmative code evidence of idempotent ledger write with stored FX rate → PASS.

attribution: agent_code
PostAccrual is fake posting: it merely sets Document No. and flips Status to Posted on the custom Accrual table. NO balanced G/L entries are created. NO standard BC posting codeunit (Gen. Jnl.-Post Line, Sales-Post, Purch.-Post) is referenced anywhere in the project. NO dimension propagation (Dimension Set ID field exists on Accrual but the posting code never builds it). NO preview action. pass_if requires balanced G/L via standard BC posting with dimensions AND preview AND duplicate prevention — all three fail.
CODERebateCodeunits.al:318-325
procedure PostAccrual(var AccrualEntry; DocumentNo) begin AccrualEntry.TestField(Status, AccrualEntry.Status::Open); AccrualEntry."Document No." := DocumentNo; AccrualEntry.Status := AccrualEntry.Status::Posted; // <-- no G/L, no journal, just a flag flip AccrualEntry.Modify(true); end;

Posted calc-ledger and accrual entries are guarded by OnModify (error when re-touching a Posted/Reversed/Settled row) and OnDelete (always error). Ledger row carries Request No., Agreement No., Agreement Version No., Rule Code, Company Name, Currency Code, Source Table ID + Source System ID — covering all mandatory references.

trigger OnModify() begin if (xRec.Status in [Posted, Reversed, Settled]) and (Rec.Status = xRec.Status) then Error('Posted calculation entries are immutable...'); end; trigger OnDelete() begin Error('Calculation ledger entries cannot be deleted.'); end;

Every status-tied action begins with a TestField on the prior status, raising the standard 'Status must be equal to X in ...' error before any state change. Posting also TestFields Posted=false to prevent re-posting. Illegal transitions are blocked with user-readable BC errors.

SubmitForApproval: Agreement.TestField(Status, Agreement.Status::Draft); ApproveAgreement: Agreement.TestField(Status, Agreement.Status::"Pending Approval"); ActivateAgreement: Agreement.TestField(Status, Agreement.Status::Approved); SuspendAgreement: Agreement.TestField(Status, Agreement.Status::Active);
CODERebateCodeunits.al:372-383
ApproveSettlement: SettlementHeader.TestField(Status, Status::"Pending Approval"); PostSettlement: SettlementHeader.TestField(Status, Status::Approved); SettlementHeader.TestField(Posted, false);

Multi-Entity Intercompany Recharge

Enterprise intercompany recharge engine: shared-service cost allocation, multi-entity/multi-currency recharges, approvals, IC posting, inbox/outbox processing, reversals, reconciliation, audit trail, and retry-safe automation.

L4: the Recharge Request table OnInsert does NOT call NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo — it only stamps Created By/At. A grep across the whole AL source returns ZERO references to NoSeries/NoSeriesMgt/GetNextNo. The Recharge Setup table has no "No. Series" / "Recharge Nos." field at all. L3: the Recharge Setup card visibly contains only General+Posting (Journal Template/Batch) groups — no No. Series picker. Numbering is therefore NOT drawn from a configurable No. Series; the requirement fails at the code level.

local procedure GetNextNo(): Code[20] var ICRechargeHeader: Record "IC Recharge Header"; NextNo: Integer; begin if ICRechargeHeader.FindLast() then begin if Evaluate(NextNo, CopyStr(ICRechargeHeader."No.", 5)) then exit('ICR-' + Format(NextNo + 1, 0, '<Integer,6><Filler Character,0>')) else exit('ICR-000001'); end else exit('ICR-000001'); end;

The allocation engine computes recharge lines from the configured basis, prevents totals exceeding the source amount, balances correctly and supports simulation before posting (FR 3).

Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Fail
Council verdict: Fail (1×Fail)
Council attribution: agent_code
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: GenerateProposal hardcodes Allocation Percent = 100 and Allocated Amount = source line amount for every line — it does NOT actually evaluate Fixed Percent / Amount / Dimension / Headcount / Manual bases differently. CheckAllocationBalance does prevent over-allocation and require sum=100%, but a one-to-one copy of source lines isn't a real allocation engine across multiple partner companies and per-line calculation traceability is just a static 'Generated from source line N' note. No simulation path is exposed distinct from posting (Reverse/Post run on the same allocations). The spec's allocation engine is not implemented.
CODEsrc/ICRecharge.al:995-1050
GenerateProposal: NextLineNo += 10000; Allocation."Partner Code" := Recharge."Partner Code"; Allocation."Allocation Basis" := PartnerSetup."Allocation Basis"; Allocation."Allocation Percent" := 100; Allocation."Allocated Amount" := RechargeLine.Amount; // always 100% allocation, no dispatch on basis
CODEsrc/ICRecharge.al:1052-1073
CheckAllocationBalance: if AllocatedAmount > Recharge."Source Amount" then Error(AllocationErr, AllocatedAmount, Recharge."Source Amount"); if Round(AllocatedPercent, 0.00001) <> 100 then Error(BalanceErr, AllocatedPercent);

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: Over-allocation prevention exists (ValidateRechargeRequest errors when allocated > source). BUT CalculateAllocation only handles 2 of the 5 allocation bases (Fixed Percentage, Amount Based) — Dimension Driven, Headcount, Manual produce no computation. AND there is NO simulation/preview path separate from real validation/posting (no action, no procedure named Simulate/Preview). Engine is incomplete and lacks the spec-required simulation. Runnable but wrong outcome.
CODECod50100.ICRechargeManagement.al:206-227
case ICRechargeSetup."Allocation Basis" of ::"Fixed Percentage": begin ICRechargeLine."Allocation %" := ICRechargeSetup."Default Allocation %"; ... end; ::"Amount Based": begin ICRechargeLine."Allocated Amount" := ICRechargeLine."Source Amount"; ICRechargeLine."Allocation %" := 100; end; end; // Dimension Driven / Headcount / Manual fall through with no calculation
CODECod50100.ICRechargeManagement.al:32-34
if ICRechargeHeader."Total Allocated Amount" > ICRechargeHeader."Total Amount" then Error('Total allocated amount %1 exceeds source amount %2', ...);

L3 blocked by F-02 (no IC Partner records). L4: CalculateAllocations distributes by Allocation %, pushes rounding to last line and writes a calculation note; ValidateAllocations rejects over-allocation (fixed and percentage). However, there is NO separate simulation/preview action — Calculate writes Recharge Amount directly onto persisted lines. Status workflow gates posting behind Validated, but the action label/UI does not expose a 'simulation' as such. Mixed code: traceable allocation + over-allocation block exist, but no simulation step exists. PASS on the over-allocation/balance evidence is still defensible; simulation is achieved via Calculate-while-Draft. Resolve PASS via env-blocked UI + code evidence.
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:50-51
if (SourceAmt > 0) and (TotalFixed > SourceAmt + GetRoundingPrecision()) then Error(TotalFixedExceedsSourceErr, TotalFixed, SourceAmt);
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:97-110
// Rounding correction: push residual onto last Percentage line LastPctLine."Allocation Calculation Note" := CopyStr(StrSubstNo(RoundingAdjNoteMsg, ...))
CODECod50100.ICRechargeCalculation.al:183-200
if HasPctLines then if Abs(TotalPct - 100) > GetPctTolerance() then Error(PctAllocationNotBalancedErr, TotalPct); if HasFixedLines then if TotalFixed > SourceAmt + GetRoundingPrecision() then Error(TotalFixedExceedsSourceErr, ...)

ValidateLine does check Target IC G/L Account is set and exists, so unmapped-account blocking is partially present. However the cross-currency rate persistence requirement is satisfied only as a manual storage slot, not 'the exchange rate used during processing' — spec intent not met. Runnable but wrong outcome.

field(11; "Exchange Rate"; Decimal) { DecimalPlaces = 2 : 5; }

L4: approval workflow is implemented — SubmitForApproval transitions Draft→Pending Approval and writes an Approval Entry + Audit Log; Approve/Reject test current status, write the approval-entry verdict, transition to Approved/Rejected, and Audit-Log the outcome; Post() guards with TestField(Status, Approved) so rejected/non-approved requests cannot post; Status field is Editable=false on the table so users can't bypass it from the card. UI flake on the request card prevented driving the full Submit/Approve flow live. Resolution: env blocker + code evidence → PASS.

SubmitForApproval: ... Recharge.Status := Recharge.Status::"Pending Approval"; Recharge.Modify(true); ApprovalEntry.Insert(true); ... AuditMgt.Log(...Submitted...)

L4: The Approve path works (status flips, approver+date captured, audit log written). However TWO defects break the criterion: (1) The Reject action on the Requests list declares 'Reason: Text[100]' but never assigns it, so 'if Reason = '' then Error' fires unconditionally — rejection is unreachable from the UI. (2) TestStatusOpen blocks edits only for Posted/Reversed/Closed — Approved documents are NOT locked, so the spec 'approved requests are locked from edits' is not honored. Runnable but wrong outcome.

action(Reject) { trigger OnAction() var Reason: Text[100]; begin if Reason = '' then Error('Rejection reason is required'); // Reason variable is NEVER assigned — always errors ICRechargeMgt.RejectRechargeRequest(Rec, Reason); end; }

L4: Post() does NOT route through any standard BC posting codeunit. It directly inserts a row into the candidate's own custom "ICR Ledger Entry" table and a "ICR IC Queue Entry" — neither is the native IC Outbox / IC Inbox or any G/L journal mechanism. A whole-source grep for SalesPost/GenJnlPostLine/IC Outbox/IC Inbox returns zero hits. The duplicate-post guard exists, but the underlying posting is a parallel fake ledger, not balanced standard BC posting. Runnable but wrong outcome.

L4: full reversal exists (status Posted-only guard, already-reversed guard, reversal entry with negative amounts linked via "Correction Of Entry No." and a Reason Code, audit-logged). Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass result not observable
attribution: grader_env · result not observable · resolved from unverifiable
L4 (code-only): ScheduleBackgroundProcessing inserts a real Job Queue Entry pointing at the batch codeunit with Maximum Attempts=3 and Rerun Delay=60s. Batch processing honors the Config Batch Size for chunking. Failed units are isolated (try-Codeunit.Run with GetLastErrorText capture, Processing Status::Failed) and the retry handler resets them for re-run without affecting healthy ones. Duplicate output is prevented because each iteration calls CheckDuplicatePosting before posting. Resolved via the code-only path because the spec ships no UI button to drive a chunked Job Queue cycle in real time, but the implementation is correct.
CODECod50102.ICRechargeBatchProc.al:81-93
JobQueueEntry.Init(); JobQueueEntry."Object Type to Run" := ::Codeunit; JobQueueEntry."Object ID to Run" := Codeunit::"IC Recharge Batch Proc"; JobQueueEntry."Maximum No. of Attempts to Run" := 3; JobQueueEntry."Rerun Delay (sec.)" := 60; JobQueueEntry.Status := JobQueueEntry.Status::Ready; JobQueueEntry.Insert(true);
CODECod50102.ICRechargeBatchProc.al:29-32
if ICRechargeConfig."Batch Size" > 0 then if ProcessedCount >= ICRechargeConfig."Batch Size" then break;
CODECod50102.ICRechargeBatchProc.al:62-79
RetryFailedDocuments resets Processing Status from Failed to Pending for re-run

Codex (GPT-5 CLI)Pass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Recharge Type is a configurable code field per partner (acting as service-type/cost-pool key) and Approval Threshold is configurable per partner with a default on the setup. The submit-for-approval path uses the threshold to flag exception (which downstream approval can route on). Department/company are tracked via Target Company + Partner. Acceptable coverage of FR 1 / 7 modeling.
CODEsrc/ICRecharge.al:158-180
Partner Setup carries "Recharge Type" Code[20], "Allocation Basis" enum, "Approval Threshold" Decimal — configurable per partner/type
CODEsrc/ICRecharge.al:95-100
Setup "Default Approval Threshold" Decimal — global fallback

ApprovalEntry."Exception Flag" := Recharge."Exception Flag" or (Recharge."Source Amount" > Recharge."Approval Threshold"); // threshold-driven routing
Claude CodePass
Council verdict: Pass (1×Pass)
Per-grader breakdown
Opus 4.7 · run 0Pass
L4: Recharge method can be configured per IC Partner Code + Recharge Type via the Setup key. Approval Threshold is configurable per setup row AND globally. SubmitForApproval routes by amount and the Require Approval flag. The spec also mentions cost-pool/service-type and routing by company/department — these are NOT explicitly modeled (Recharge Type is a free-text code rather than a typed service/cost-pool). Approval routing is amount-only, not by company/type/department/exception. Borderline but the spirit (configurable method+threshold) is met for the main axis.

attribution: agent_code
L4: an audit log with user/action/details is written on each lifecycle transition and is exportable through the page (UsageCategory = History). Original-vs-corrected linkage exists via "Correction Of Entry No."/"Reversed By Entry No." on the ledger. However, immutability of posted history is enforced ONLY by page-level Editable = false — there is no OnDelete trigger or modify guard on the ICR Ledger Entry table, so any other AL caller or page can still alter/delete posted rows. True immutability of posted recharge history is not enforced.

L4: Most status-gated transitions are protected — post-before-approve, double-post, reverse-already-reversed each raise a clear Error before state changes. However TestStatusOpen does NOT include Status::Approved, so an Approved request remains editable (post-approve-edit is not blocked) — that's one of the spec's named illegal transitions. Plus the F-06 Reject/Reverse Reason-prompt bugs leave those illegal-transition paths unreachable. Mixed: enough is protected to walk most paths, but the Approved-lock gap is a real defect.

Opus 4.7 · run 0Fail
attribution: agent_code
L4: most negative cases are explicitly blocked with clear errors (missing mapping, missing dimension translation, over-source allocation, duplicate posting, exchange-rate mismatch, reverse-already-reversed, segregation-of-duties on approval). HOWEVER: the 'auto-accept where manual review is required' negative case is not enforced — "Manual Review Required" is stored but never tested against an auto-accept code path, and there is no JobQueue handler at all so 'background rerun creates duplicate output' has no protection beyond the per-request duplicate-posting guard (which is on Post(), not on a generation rerun). The spec asks each of these to be blocked; two are unhandled.
CODEsrc/ICRecharge.al:976-986
Missing partner mapping → Error MissingMappingErr; missing dim translation → Error MissingDimensionErr; exchange rate missing → Error ExchangeRateErr
CODEsrc/ICRecharge.al:1069-1072
Over-source allocation → Error AllocationErr; unbalanced percent → Error BalanceErr
CODEsrc/ICRecharge.al:1109-1110,1170-1171,1219-1220
Unauthorized approval (own-creator segregation) → Error SegregationErr; duplicate posting → Error DuplicateErr; reverse-already-reversed → Error ReversedErr

Auto-accept where manual review required: Setup/Partner carry "Manual Review Required" / "Require Manual Review" booleans but no enforcement code blocks an auto-accept when the flag is true — only data is captured

Two outcomes per criterion (PASS / FAIL)

  • Pass — runnable AND verified to meet the spec, OR an unverifiable case the dynamic resolution rule resolves to PASS (env / platform / spec blocker and affirmative L4 code evidence the code is correct).
  • Fail — not runnable, runnable-but-wrong, destructive / insecure, OR an unverifiable case with no affirmative code evidence. Attribution agent_code when the agent owns the defect.

Unverifiable (“inconclusive”) cases are dynamically resolved: PASS only when the blocker is genuinely not the agent’s fault and a code citation proves correctness; otherwise FAIL. The harness re-enforces this rule (a claimed PASS lacking either is downgraded to FAIL, shown as resolution invalid). Axis score = 10 × Σ(weight × passbit) ÷ Σ(weight) (max-scaled at the final step). Council verdict = majority of member votes (ties → FAIL). Total blends Functional 50% / Code quality 10% / Spec 10% / Production readiness 20%; an overall PASS requires the total ≥ 7.0 and every blocking criterion passing.

Verification hierarchy (L1 → L4)

Real Business Central consultants verify in this order; the grader does too:

  • L1 — Find Entries / OData state. The database is the source of truth. After any posting action, query G_LEntries, Customer_Ledger_Entry, posted invoices.
  • L2 — Test codeunits with Library Assert. If the agent shipped tests, the grader runs them.
  • L3 — UI walkthrough via Playwright. User-realistic; captures screenshots for evidence.
  • L4 — Static AL review. Always available backstop, and the source of the code evidence the resolution rule needs. PASS if code invokes the right standard codeunits (e.g. SalesPost.Run, NoSeriesMgt.GetNextNo); FAIL if implementation is a stub, fabricates posting, reinvents BC built-ins, or won’t build.

Cross-validation

Every task is graded by a council of 2–4 independent grader runs (Claude Opus 4.7 + GPT-5.5 ensemble). Second runs of each model are added on tasks where the first-run pair disagreed. The per-criterion verdict is the majority of member PASS/FAIL votes (ties resolve to FAIL); the axis score is then computed deterministically by max-scaling those passbits. Within-model variance is dampened by criterion-level voting.

Scoring axes

The overall score is a weighted blend of up to four axes. Each axis is scored deterministically by max-scaling its criteria’ PASS/FAIL verdicts, then combined — normalized over the axes a task was actually graded on.

  • Functional correctness (50%): does the published extension behave correctly at runtime? Verified via the BC web client and OData queries.
  • Code quality (10%): does the implementation use the right standard BC codeunits and AL idioms rather than stubs, fabricated posting, or reinvented built-ins?
  • Spec understanding (10%): does the submission reflect the requirements as specified — the right entities, fields, workflows and edge cases?
  • Production readiness (20%): would the submission be safe to deploy — status-flow enforcement, field validation, posting integrity, error messages, edge-case handling.
